body:
This PR fixes the CUDA compat, until we get #2199 working, and upgrades the dependencies (to use the latest ClimaTimeSteppers).

I already ran CI with the latest ClimaTimeSteppers branch here: https://buildkite.com/clima/climaatmos-ci/builds/13626 and results looked like they match within machine precision, so I'm just going to increment the ref counter and r+.
title: Fix CUDA compat, upgrade deps

body:
Content
- Splits up `AtmosConfig` constructor, for a clearer flow and dispatch on `Dicts`, `Strings` (filepath) and `Nothing`. This allows for circumventing the CLI and adds another entry point in the config generation flow. This is technically a breaking change, and might need to be changed before merging. 
- Add `perf/common.jl` with two new functions to easily generate configurations for performance jobs. `AtmosCoveragePerfConfig` takes in a config dictionary, overrides the default performance configuration, and passes it to `AtmosConfig`. `TargetJobConfig` takes in a job id, and constructs its corresponding `AtmosConfig` 

- Rework `flame.jl`, `jet_test_nfailures.jl`, and `benchmark.jl` to maintain continuity for the perf tests. Apply this change for the relevant buildkite jobs in `pipeline.yml`.
- Add tests for `AtmosCoveragePerfConfig`
title: Simplify configurations for performance jobs
Config files for multi-year, high-topped atmosphere simulation with clearksy radiation

body: This PR lowers the allocation limits
title: Lower allocation limits

body:
HDF5 has many many runtime dispatch warnings in JET, and while I do think that some of them could be fixed, IIRC others are unavoidable due to working with generic types. So, this PR ignores that module to reduce noise in the JET report.

A build of failures are here: https://buildkite.com/clima/climaatmos-ci/builds/13072#018ab62d-8411-4cf8-a1b9-c29547cdef3b
title: Ignore HDF5 in JET reports

body: This PR removes the dss callback, which is never used (the timestepper calls `dss!`)-- we now kind of depend on these post-explicit and post-implicit callback hooks, so there's probably a few more things that we can clean up. Also, I don't think it's likely that we can ever go back to using OrdinaryDiffEq with these requirements, so we can probably remove some of that code, too.
title: Remove dss callback
